speaker: Brené Brown
episode_or_content_title: Daring Greatly: How the Courage to Be Vulnerable Transforms the Way We Live, Love, Parent, and Lead (2012)
verification_notes: Created 2026-05-26 via the Six-State Buildout (Phase 8 human-gated promotion). prototype_only reconstruction — paraphrase of the speaker's documented idea; NOT a verified verbatim excerpt. Brené Brown is Professor of Research at University of Houston Graduate College of Social Work, author of six NYT bestsellers, and holder of one of the top five most- viewed TED talks. Daring Greatly (2012, Gotham Books) is her canonical work on vulnerability as courage. Tier-1 (academic researcher + major public platform). The "vulnerability as uncertainty, risk, and emotional exposure" definition and the "wholehearted" framing are documented in Daring Greatly and corroborated in multiple official secondary sources. Official URL confirmed: https://brenebrown.com/book/daring-greatly/. No transcript fetched (book source). Paraphrase is the honest label; the claim is thoroughly documented in a real, verifiable book by this author.
